attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

[] )\\n            .filter( w => w != null && w.Type !== FeatureWheelSegmentType.None );\\n\\n        if ( features.length === 0 )\\n        {\\n            // 不應發生（守衛已擋）；fail-loud 但不炸狀態機，直接續流程\\n            console.warn( \\\"[FeatureWheelShowState] 無有效 WheelFeatures，跳過轉輪演出\\\" );\\n            stateManager.NextState( Game_Define.GameState.ROUND_SHOW_END );\\n            return;\\n        }\\n\\n        // eventManager.Dispatch( FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.SHOW_UI );\\n\\n        // WheelCount 迴圈：逐項「序列等待」——PlaySpin 有 m_isSpinning 重入守衛，\\n        // 未等前一次 callback 就發下一次會被靜默丟棄（FeatureWheelManager 行為，BC-2 硬約束）。\\n        for ( const wf of features )\\n        {\\n            const landIndex = wf.SlotIndex ?? 0;\\n\\n            // 防 hang：PlaySpin 對越界 landIndex 是 warn+return（不呼叫 callback）→ 若 await 會永久掛住。\\n            // 故在 dispatch 前先驗範圍，越界則 fail-loud 跳過該次（不 await）。\\n            if ( landIndex < 0 || landIndex >= FEATURE_WHEEL_SEGMENT_COUNT )\\n            {\\n                console.warn( `[FeatureWheelShowState] SlotIndex=${landIndex} 越界 0~${FEATURE_WHEEL_SEGMENT_COUNT - 1}，跳過該次轉動` );\\n                continue;\\n            }\\n\\n            const result = await this.PlaySpinOnce( landIndex );\\n\\n            // fail-loud 一致性：落點 type 應與 server WheelFeatures.Type 相符\\n            // （前提 P：server SlotIndex 與客戶端 12 格序一致——待 server 對拍；不符僅 warn 不阻流程）\\n            if ( result != null && result.type !== wf.Type )\\n            {\\n                console.warn( `[FeatureWheelShowState] 落點 type 不一致：landed=${result.type} vs server=${wf.Type}（SlotIndex=${landIndex}）` );\\n            }\\n\\n            // ②標記 seam：通知演出端在盤面標記此功能（標記/icon/乘倍/Grand報獎/FreeGame盤外 由同事 handler 渲染）。\\n            // 暫 fire-and-forget（不 await，無 handler 不 hang）；待 handler 上線再視需要改 await 排序。詳見 FeatureMarkEvent.ts。\\n            eventManager.Dispatch( FEATURE_MARK_EVENT.SHOW_MARK, wf.Type, wf.Positions ?? [], wf.Multipliers ?? [] );\\n        }\\n\\n        // eventManager.Dispatch( FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.HIDE_UI );\\n\\n        stateManager.NextState( Game_Define.GameState.ROUND_SHOW_END );\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 包一層 Promise 等單次 PLAY_SPIN 完成回調\\n     * @param landIndex 落點格索引（已由呼叫端確認 0~11 範圍）\\n     */\\n    private PlaySpinOnce( landIndex: number ): Promise<SpinResult | null>\\n    {\\n        return new Promise<SpinResult | null>( resolve =>\\n        {\\n            eventManager.Dispatch(\\n                FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.PLAY_SPIN,\\n                landIndex,\\n                ( result: SpinResult ) => resolve( result ?? null )\\n            );\\n        } );\\n    }\\n\\n    public OnProcess()\\n    {\\n\\n    }\\n\\n    public OnLeave()\\n    {\\n\\n    }\\n}\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[{\"oldStart\":80,\"oldLines\":6,\"newStart\":80,\"newLines\":13,\"lines\":[\" \",\"         // eventManager.Dispatch( FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.HIDE_UI );\",\" \",\"+        // ③轉換 seam：標記後、進 respin 前，套用符號轉換（一般符號→CASH）。fire-and-forget；演出由同事 handler 渲染。\",\"+        const transferResult = this.m_gameView.RoundInfo?.TransferResult ?? <observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Added remaining feature wheel event seams</title>
  <subtitle>Extended FeatureMarkEvent with APPLY_TRANSFER and SHOW_SCATTER_DROP events for symbol conversion and scatter animation</subtitle>

    <fact>APPLY_TRANSFER event handles seam ③ for symbol conversion (normal symbols→CASH) after marking but before entering respin</fact>
    <fact>APPLY_TRANSFER payload contains RoundInfo.TransferResult with Pos, Symbol, Number fields</fact>
    <fact>SHOW_SCATTER_DROP event handles seam ⑨ for scatter drop animation when wheel lands FreeGame before entering FG mode</fact>
    <fact>SHOW_SCATTER_DROP has no payload as presentation layer generates animation based on current board state</fact>
    <fact>Both events follow fire-and-forget pattern allowing handlers to be implemented later without blocking state machine</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Extended FeatureMarkEvent enum with two additional event seam points to complete the feature wheel integration contract. APPLY_TRANSFER (seam ③) fires after wheel marking but before respin entry, passing TransferResult data for symbol conversion effects (normal→CASH). SHOW_SCATTER_DROP (seam ⑨) fires when the wheel lands on FreeGame type before entering free game mode, allowing the presentation layer to animate scatter symbols dropping onto the board; this event has no payload as the animation is generated from current board state. Both events maintain the fire-and-forget dispatch pattern, ensuring state machine flow continues without waiting for presentation handlers that may not yet be implemented.</narrative>

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Integrated symbol transfer event into wheel show state</title>
  <subtitle>FeatureWheelShowState now dispatches APPLY_TRANSFER after marking completes to trigger symbol conversion presentation</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FeatureWheelShowState extracts TransferResult from RoundInfo after all wheel spins and marking complete</fact>
    <fact>APPLY_TRANSFER event dispatched with transferResult payload when array length exceeds zero</fact>
    <fact>Event fires after HIDE_UI comment and before transitioning to ROUND_SHOW_END state</fact>
    <fact>Uses fire-and-forget dispatch pattern allowing presentation handler to be implemented independently</fact>
    <fact>Implements seam ③ for symbol conversion (normal symbols→CASH) before entering respin</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Integrated seam ③ symbol transfer event into FeatureWheelShowState's exit flow. After all wheel spins complete and marking events fire, the state now extracts RoundInfo.TransferResult and conditionally dispatches APPLY_TRANSFER when transfer data exists. The event fires after wheel UI would hide but before transitioning to ROUND_SHOW_END, positioning symbol conversion presentation logically between wheel completion and respin entry. The fire-and-forget dispatch pattern ensures state flow continues without waiting for presentation handlers, maintaining the separation of concerns where logic layer defines timing while presentation layer implements effects independently.</narrative>
